The obligation to appease the wrongly accused: Eli must make peace with Hannah
The obligation to bless one wrongly suspected: Eli's blessing to Hannah

Hannah's conditional threat: If you see, good; if not, I will manipulate the Sotah process
The debate over the Sotah waters: Does the barren woman become pregnant or does the woman with pain childbirth ease?

The problem of excess: When the lion is satisfied, he roars; prosperity leads to rebellion
The parable of the spoiled son: When you give too much, sin is inevitable

macro_segments.title: Moses's Prayer for Israel: Holding God Accountable and Defending the People

micro_segments.title: God's initial response: Go down, you have lost your status because I'm angry at Israel

Moses grabbing God by the collar: The power of prayer to compel divine mercy
Moses's refusal of replacement: God offers to make him a great nation; Moses insists on Israel

Moses invokes the oath to the patriarchs: God cannot nullify a promise sworn in His own eternal name
Moses's pragmatic argument: The nations will say God is weak if He destroys Israel after the miracles and conquest
God concedes to Moses: 'I have forgiven them according to your words'

macro_segments.title: The Structure of Prayer: Praise Before Request

micro_segments.title: The ruling of Rav Simai: One must always praise God before making requests
The source from Moses: The example of Deuteronomy where Moses praises before requesting entry to the land
